semanticCore: Plucking fruit, harvest bunch, low hanging, pace, or gather
quranicRelevance: Low-hanging fruit clusters of Paradise (Qutoof - 'qutoofuha daniyatun').

keyDerivations.meaning: Harvested fruit bunch, low-hanging cluster of grapes/dates, or plucked branch

figurativeOrRareUsages: فِي جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَةٍ ﴿٢٢﴾ قُطُوفُهَا دَانِيَةٌ (69:22-23): In a high garden, its harvested clusters/fruits hanging low (effortless abundance).
summaryEn: The root represents harvest clusters (Qutoof), detailing the effortless, low-hanging fruits of Paradise that require no climbing or physical labor to reach.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر جني الثمار وقطع العناقيد الدانية (قطف)، واصفاً سهولة نيل طعام الجنة حيث عناقيد فواكهها قريبة متذللة للقطف (قطوفها دانية).
rawText: قَطَائِفُ : see my 1001 Nights, note 23 to chap. viii. See also زَلاَبِيَه. In the TA, art. كنف, it is applied to كُنَافَة. قَطَافٌ and 🡻قِطَافٌ The time of gathering the crop of grapes: (S, Mgh, K:) or the latter has this meaning; and the former is allowable accord. to Ks: (T, TA:) and the latter is also an inf. n., (Mgh,) or may be so, (Ks, T, TA,) meaning the gathering of the crop of grapes: (Mgh:) [or both have this meaning; for] you say, هٰذَا زَمَانُ القَطَافِ and القِطَافِ. (Msb.) See جَدَادٌ. قَطَفٌ : see بَقْلٌ. قَطِيفٌ a coll. gen. n. syn. with قَطَائِفُ, mentioned in the TA voce أُبْلُوجٌ, which see. === قَطِيفَةٌ A villous, or nappy, دِثَار [or outer wrapping garment]. (S, Msb, K.) See also راَحُولَاتٌ. قِطْفٌ A bunch of grapes, &c.: pl. قُطُوفٌ: see an ex. voce ذَلَّلَ. === قِطْفٌ i. q. مَقْطُوفٌ. (TA in art. بسط.) See بَسِيطٌ. مِقْطَفٌ (vulg. مَقْطَفٌ) [pl. مَقَاطِف] A handbasket, made of palm-leaves: so called because originally used in gathering fruit. (See also قُفَّة and زَنْبِيل.)
